body:
### Overview

This control identifies cases where users without MFA or 2-step verification have access that can reach datastores. The checks include AWS IAM users with console passwords and no MFA, Google and Google Cloud users without 2SV, Entra or Azure users with role assignments and no MFA, and Alibaba RAM users with login profiles that do not require MFA.

Datastore access should require strong authentication because these identities can reach sensitive data.
name: Ensure Users With Access to Datastores Have MFA/2SV
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Require MFA or 2-step verification for every user that can access datastore resources. This control identifies users that still have datastore-relevant access while MFA/2SV is not active.

### AWS

1. Open **IAM > Users** and select the affected IAM user.
2. Confirm the user has a console password and datastore access directly or through groups, roles, or policies.
3. Open **Security credentials** and assign an MFA device to the user.
4. If the user no longer needs datastore access, remove the related IAM policies or group memberships instead of only enabling MFA.
5. Prefer IAM Identity Center and role-based access for ongoing admin or datastore access.
6. Validate with the AWS CLI:

~~~bash
aws iam list-mfa-devices --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name {{asset.name}}
aws iam list-groups-for-user --user-name {{asset.name}}
~~~

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_mfa_enable_virtual.html

The control is satisfied when affected IAM users with console passwords have MFA active or no longer have datastore access.

### GCP

1. Identify the Google identity behind the affected Google Cloud IAM user.
2. In Google Admin, open **Security > Authentication > 2-step verification**.
3. Enforce 2-Step Verification for the user's organizational unit or group.
4. Remove the user's datastore role bindings if access is no longer required.
5. Validate project, folder, or organization IAM bindings for the user:

~~~bash
gcloud projects get-iam-policy <project-id> \
  --flatten="bindings[].members" \
  --filter="bindings.members:<user-email>"
~~~

Reference: https://support.google.com/a/answer/9176657

The control is satisfied when datastore-access Google Cloud users are enrolled in 2SV or no longer have datastore access.

### Azure

1. Identify the affected Microsoft Entra user and the Azure datastore role assignment path.
2. Open **Microsoft Entra ID > Protection > Conditional Access**.
3. Create or update a policy that includes the affected users or privileged datastore access groups.
4. Target Azure management and relevant database/storage apps, then require **Multifactor authentication**.
5. Remove direct datastore role assignments if they are no longer required.
6. Validate the user's MFA state and role assignments:

~~~bash
az role assignment list --assignee <user-object-id> --all
~~~

References:
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/tutorial-enable-azure-mfa
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/howto-conditional-access-policy-all-users-mfa

The control is satisfied when affected Entra users with Azure datastore access have MFA active or no longer have the datastore role assignment.

### Alibaba

1. Open **RAM > Users** and select the affected RAM user.
2. Confirm the user has a console login profile and access to datastore services such as RDS, OSS, or Tablestore.
3. Bind an MFA device to the RAM user.
4. Remove datastore policies or group memberships if the user no longer requires access.
5. Validate the user MFA configuration with Alibaba Cloud CLI:

~~~bash
aliyun ram GetUser --UserName <user-name>
aliyun ram ListVirtualMFADevices
~~~

Reference: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/en/ram/user-guide/enable-an-mfa-device-for-a-ram-user

The control is satisfied when affected RAM users with console access require MFA or no longer have datastore access.
description: Users without MFA or 2-step verification that have access to datastores.
